$comment: Mossen Distribution Contract v1 — machine-readable single source of truth for the public API surface that cli-harness and other integrators depend on. Edited only via PR; every PR that changes stable_surface must update this file in the same commit (wave_w442 smoke enforces this).

$changelog.1.0.1: R8 §3 patch — annotate hook_event subtype variants + permission_denial real location (embedded in result.permission_denials[]); add ndjson_top_level_extras section. Backward-compatible documentation enrichment, no surface change.
$changelog.1.0.0: Initial contract per Phase 1 W442. cli-harness R7 §5 sign-off; final GA tag locked at v1.0.0 after R10 sign-off.

$mossenVersionHistory.1.1.0: W458 (user-facing model alias scrub) + W459 (first-launch LLM profile wizard) + W459-full (provider picker + URL validation + endpoint test + multi-profile loop + MemorySidecarWizardStep) + W459-schema (per-profile env-var apiKey reference via new ProfileApiKeyEnvRef type) + W462 (/permissions mode-vs-rule clarification). Contract surface unchanged from 1.0.1 — all changes are additive UI/onboarding/internals; no breaking change for cli-harness or other integrators.
$mossenVersionHistory.1.0.0: GA release. Distribution Plan v1 all 4 phases. Promoted from v1.0.0-rc2 after cli-harness R10 sign-off.

ndjson_message_envelope.$comment: Top-level mossen SDK messages observed on the NDJSON stream. The `type` field discriminates; some message types (notably 'system') use a `subtype` field for sub-discrimination. R4 §B.3 lists 14 short event-type names; this section maps them to the actual {type, subtype?} pairs in mossen source.

ndjson_stream_event_subtypes.$comment: When type='stream_event', the nested event.type discriminates between content delta kinds. R4 §B.3 lists these as top-level event names; cli-harness extracts them from the stream_event envelope.
ndjson_stream_event_subtypes.tool_result: embedded in user message content block (type='tool_result')
ndjson_stream_event_subtypes.error: via result message subtype='error' OR as stderr line
ndjson_stream_event_subtypes.hook_event: R8-resolved (2026-05-25): emitted as system messages with one of three subtypes — {type:'system', subtype:'hook_started'|'hook_progress'|'hook_response'}. See cli/print.ts:823-868 + entrypoints/sdk/coreSchemas.ts SDKHookStartedMessageSchema / SDKHookProgressMessageSchema / SDKHookResponseMessageSchema. cli-harness R8 §1: cli-harness sdk was filtering on top-level type='hook_event' — dead code, cli-harness will fix.
ndjson_stream_event_subtypes.permission_denial: R8-resolved (2026-05-25): NOT a top-level event type. Emitted as elements of result.permission_denials[] (SDKPermissionDenial[] array) on the final result message. See QueryEngine.ts (8 push-sites) + ndjson_top_level_extras.result_permission_denials below for the shape. cli-harness R8 §2: cli-harness sdk was filtering on top-level type='permission_denial' — dead code, cli-harness will fix.

ndjson_top_level_extras.$comment: Side-channel data attached to the result message envelope (not its own top-level type). Documented here so contract consumers know where to find them rather than searching for missing top-level event types.

element_fields.toolInput: object (the tool input that was denied)

result_permission_denials.emit_sites: QueryEngine.ts — 8 push-sites on the in-flight result accumulator
result_permission_denials.consumer_note: cli-harness Memory pane / permission audit log iterates this array after stream completion to surface denied operations to the user. R8 §2 confirms this is the correct integration point.

system_init_fields.$comment: W442 dual-emit: snake_case (deprecated_since 1.0.0, removed in 1.2.0) and camelCase (stable). Both forms emit simultaneously during the deprecation window. R4 §B.3 (camelCase) is the long-term contract.

plugins.type: { name: string, path: string, source?: string }[]

memory_file_fields.$comment: Per R7 §2: cli-harness MemoryPane.tsx displays all 6 fields. No trimming.

type.type: string (one of: User, Project, Local, Managed, AutoMem, TeamMem)

data_paths.$comment: cli_harness_ownership_prefix is owned exclusively by cli-harness — mossen must NOT read or write under this prefix. settings_active_profile_key value is contract-locked per R4 §B.5.

exit_codes.$comment: INFORMATIONAL: W442 records the target contract; mossen source currently uses only 0/1/2/143. Phase 4 W453 will refactor process.exit callsites to use this enum. cli-harness should defer exit-code classification migration until Phase 4 ships.

tool_result_meta_shapes.$comment: INFORMATIONAL: W442 records the target contract; mossen source does not yet emit tool_result._meta. Phase 4 W454 will add structured _meta to these 4 tools. Until then cli-harness keeps the 3 summary-regex sites (per R7 §1). Removing the regexes is gated on W454.

deprecated_surface.$comment: Fields emitted by current mossen source that will be removed in a future minor release. Per Distribution Plan §6.2 deprecation policy: at least 2 minor cycles between deprecated-marking and removal.

breaking_change_protocol.policy: Any change that removes or renames a field in stable_surface must be preceded by: (a) a deprecation entry in deprecated_surface for at least 2 minor cycles, (b) a 'BREAKING:' line in CHANGELOG.md, (c) a major bump of contractVersion if the schema itself changes. R5 Q3 policy locked.

internals_do_not_depend_on: Mossen internal package layout (services/, utils/, etc.)
messaging_socket_path / messagingSocketPath value (internal UDS path, only present when UDS_INBOX feature flag is on)
